title: Verify failure handling and cleanup workflow
description:
## Objective
Prove an owner can recover from or safely terminate every failed ingestion without payload disclosure, source damage, or Ledger deletion.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION: Protect local statement and ingestion data (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP-CONTRACT: Verify abandon and artifact cleanup (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-FAILURE-STATUS-CONTRACT: Verify safe failure and status reporting (verifies).
- Proves or constrains UC-INGEST-005: Handle and clean up a failed ingestion (covers).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC verification exercises published status/abandon/cleanup operations.
- bd-114 (TASK-INGEST-GATE-SECURITY): The workflow consumes the consolidated security canary and permission evidence.

## In Scope
- Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-005 diagnostics, durable status, retry/resume/abandon decisions, and state-checked cleanup.
- Produce: UCIngest005Evidence.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Every failure returns stable code/category, safe IDs, mutation possibility, last durable state, and retry/resume/abandon/correct-source guidance with one stdout result and metadata-only stderr.
- When payload would be needed to explain an error, the result uses safe correlation/record IDs instead and canary data is absent from every output/log.
- Permission-establishment failure persists no sensitive artifact and performs no Ledger mutation.
- Status proves pre-commit failures have no committable manifest/effect and commit-stage failures preserve exact completed outcomes plus unresolved frontier.
- A simulated crash leaves only known durable/recoverable artifacts; next safe startup removes disposable residue and retains required state.
- Cleanup rejects ambiguous/locked/recoverable targets; abandon prevents commit/resume but preserves prior effects; terminal cleanup removes only selected INGEST artifacts and leaves source/Ledger unchanged.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT inspect/delete files directly from the test except through isolated setup/observation helpers; invoke public abandon/cleanup for behavior.
- ❌ Do NOT emit private payload when a case fails.

## Approach
Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-005 diagnostics, durable status, retry/resume/abandon decisions, and state-checked cleanup.
- Constraint: Filesystem observations are restricted to isolated test data roots and source hashes; cleanup never targets the repository fixture directory.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: IngestSecurityGateEvidence (NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION)
- Produces: UCIngest005Evidence (UC-INGEST-005)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/E2E/FailureCleanupWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — UC-INGEST-005 black-box gate — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~FailureCleanupWorkflowTests` -> exit 0; at least 14 diagnostic, permission, pre/post-mutation status, crash residue, retained-for-recovery, abandon, terminal cleanup, source, and Ledger-preservation cases are discovered and 0 tests fail

## Commit

title: Validate INGEST local financial-data security
description:
## Objective
Prove that hostile/malformed input and every success/failure/crash path disclose no source payload and leave only authorized protected state.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY: Memory-only extraction and owner-only payload handling (governed-by).
- Proves or constrains DD-INGEST-DOCUMENT-EXTRACTION: Managed in-process PDF extraction with PdfPig (governed-by).
- Proves or constrains DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE: Separate raw-SQLite ingestion state with retention compaction (governed-by).
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-BOUNDED-PARSING: Bound passive statement processing (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION: Protect local statement and ingestion data (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains RISK-INGEST-004: Raw statements or extracted temporary data can leak through permissions, logs, crash artifacts, or indefinite retention. (references).
- Proves or constrains RISK-INGEST-007: A malformed or adversarial document can exhaust local CPU, memory, disk, decompression, or parser time, or attempt active-content and external-resource execution. (references).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-ARTIFACT-PROTECTION: Verify source and artifact privacy boundaries (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION-AOT: Verify PdfPig Native-AOT extraction conformance (verifies).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Security is validated against the complete published eight-operation surface.
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): Consumes PrivateStatementFixtureSet.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): Consumes IngestArtifactProtection.EnsureOwnerOnly.

## In Scope
- Security gate intentionally has no Implements link: it consolidates personal financial payload, passive parser, permissions, source preservation, diagnostics, cleanup, and resource-bound obligations across the published module.
- Produce: IngestSecurityGateEvidence.
- Produce: Published security boundary tests.
- Produce: Malformed/resource canary tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Published preview receives sourcePath only through private stdin/file JSON; process command line, environment diagnostics, stdout errors, stderr, logs, crash output, and telemetry contain none of the fixture path, canary, row, description, amount, balance, full identifier, request, manifest, or receipt payload.
- INGEST directories are 0700 and database/WAL/SHM/lock/atomic files are 0600 where supported; permission-establishment failure returns stable permission error before sensitive persistence or Ledger mutation.
- Before/after SHA-256 proves every private source unchanged across preview success, malformed/unsupported/resource failure, approval, commit, resume, abandonment, cleanup, cancellation, and injected crash.
- Malformed, scan-only, password-required, ambiguous, active-content/external-reference, decompression, nesting, byte, page, record, memory, and wall-time cases fail closed before mutation and start no network listener, child process, renderer, OCR, or remote fetch.
- Normal extraction creates no temporary; crash/startup cleanup removes only known unlocked disposable INGEST artifacts and leaves no orphan extraction temporary.
- Architecture guards reject HTTP/FastEndpoints/Aspire/PostgreSQL/EF, reflection/plugin loading, private Ledger storage/handler references, payload logging, and source argument options.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT weaken canary assertions, redact after logging, or treat process isolation as sufficient — prevent payload entry at each boundary.
- ❌ Do NOT skip owner-only mode checks on supported hosts or continue after failure.
- ❌ Do NOT persist test fixture paths/content or emit them when a security assertion fails.

## Approach
Security gate intentionally has no Implements link: it consolidates personal financial payload, passive parser, permissions, source preservation, diagnostics, cleanup, and resource-bound obligations across the published module.
- Constraint: Security failures themselves must use metadata-only stable identifiers and never echo the triggering payload.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Published INGEST surface
- Consumes: IngestArtifactProtection.EnsureOwnerOnly (DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY) — Permission enforcement
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT) — External private fixture injection
- Consumes: PublishedIngestTallyFixture (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Published process boundary
- Produces: IngestSecurityGateEvidence (NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION) — Consolidated security proof

- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Security/IngestResourceCanaryTests.cs` (test) — malformed/bounds/payload canaries — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Process/PublishedIngestSecurityTests.cs` (test) — process arguments/streams/network/orphan gate — required

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~IngestSecurityBoundaryTests|FullyQualifiedName~IngestResourceCanaryTests|FullyQualifiedName~PublishedIngestSecurityTests'` -> exit 0; at least 28 permission, canary, source-hash, malformed/resource, temporary, offline, process, stream, architecture, and crash cases are discovered and 0 tests fail without echoing canary payloads

title: Implement the qualified layout A adapter
description:
## Objective
Produce deterministic layout A statement evidence and normalized source records without a generic template or fallback parser.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-FINANCIAL-NORMALIZATION: Normalize exact source financial facts
  - AC: Given a transaction-bearing source record with complete evidence, when it is normalized, then the candidate preserves the selected account, original description, exact base-10 amount, ZAR, transaction date, optional distinct posting date, adapter version, source fingerprint, source-context record identity, and source reference without precision loss.
  - AC: Given an asset or liability statement movement, when its signed amount is normalized, then positive means the owner economic position improved and negative means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
  - AC: Given a source transaction date that includes its year, when it is normalized, then that exact calendar date is the Effective Date.
  - AC: Given a source row with zero monetary movement, when it is accounted for, then it is retained as an explicitly excluded non-transaction record with a stable reason and no zero-amount LEDGER candidate is created.
  - AC: Given a missing or ambiguous amount, sign, currency, date, description, record boundary, account meaning, or provenance field, when normalization is attempted, then the source record is blocked without inventing or editing a value.
  - AC: Given a row date without a year in the qualified yearless layout, when exactly one matching calendar date falls inside the explicit statement period, then that date is the Effective Date; otherwise the batch is blocked.
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION: Qualify and detect supported statement variants
  - AC: Given any of the three qualified private fixtures, when variant detection runs, then exactly one of the two versioned text-native PDF variants is selected from structural evidence rather than filename or extension.
  - AC: Given the qualified permission-encrypted fixture that is text-extractable without an opening password, when it is previewed twice, then each run selects exactly the fixture manifest variantId, reproduces every expected ordered record and reconciliation control, requires no opening password, OCR, external process, or external fetch, completes within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory, and creates no LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a scan-only PDF, opening-password requirement, unsupported tabular export, active-content dependency, external fetch, malformed structure, ambiguous variant, or changed layout, when detection runs, then INGEST returns a stable unsupported or unsafe-source result and creates no committable manifest or LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a source exceeding any advertised adapter bound for size, pages, records, nesting, decompression, memory, or processing time, when preview is attempted, then processing stops with a stable resource-limit result before mutation.
  - AC: Given a proposed new or revised adapter, when it lacks representative fixtures that account for every source record and reconcile every required financial control deterministically, then the variant cannot be advertised as supported.

## Depends On
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): The adapter implements IStatementAdapter over PdfDocumentEvidence and uses the private fixture helper.

## In Scope
- Encode only the structural detection, row boundaries, financial semantics, exclusions, and source controls proven by the private layout A fixtures.
- Produce: PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter.
- Produce: LayoutAStatementAdapterTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Descriptor advertises exactly pdf-text-layout-a-v1, its adapter/extraction/manifest versions, media type, and hard limits.
- Probe selects exact_match only from layout A structural evidence and returns no_match for every layout B and unsupported fixture; filename and extension never contribute.
- Extract accounts for every layout A source record in source order, preserves raw evidence privately, distinguishes transaction and non-transaction records, and exposes all variant controls.
- Layout A date, account-class, owner-economic sign, zero-movement exclusion, amount, description, source-reference, and statement-period rules reproduce every private expected fact exactly in integer minor units.
- Two extraction runs over every assigned fixture produce identical ordered evidence and stable source-context positions; malformed or drifted structures fail closed.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add conditional branches for layout B or future banks — rejected single-parser alternative per DD-INGEST-FORMAT-ADAPTERS.
- ❌ Do NOT use filename, extension, fuzzy matching, user-authored regex/template configuration, or fallback guesses.
- ❌ Do NOT commit private fixtures, expected facts, filenames, extracted text, or payload snapshots.

## Approach
Encode only the structural detection, row boundaries, financial semantics, exclusions, and source controls proven by the private layout A fixtures.
- Constraint: All private expected facts come through PrivateStatementFixtureSet and remain outside source control.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: IStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PdfDocumentEvidence (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT)
- Produces: PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf/PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter.cs` (create) — layout A probe and extraction — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Adapters/LayoutAStatementAdapterTests.cs` (test) — private golden-fixture tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~LayoutAStatementAdapterTests` -> exit 0; at least 10 probe, drift, row-accounting, financial-fact, control, and repeatability cases are discovered for every layout A fixture and 0 tests fail

## Commit

- NFR-INGEST-DETERMINISTIC-INTEGRITY

title: Implement bounded in-process PDF extraction
description:
## Objective
Extract deterministic passive PDF evidence under Native-AOT and resource bounds without source mutation, child processes, disk extraction, or a speculative parser port.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION: Qualify and detect supported statement variants
  - AC: Given any of the three qualified private fixtures, when variant detection runs, then exactly one of the two versioned text-native PDF variants is selected from structural evidence rather than filename or extension.
  - AC: Given the qualified permission-encrypted fixture that is text-extractable without an opening password, when it is previewed twice, then each run selects exactly the fixture manifest variantId, reproduces every expected ordered record and reconciliation control, requires no opening password, OCR, external process, or external fetch, completes within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory, and creates no LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a scan-only PDF, opening-password requirement, unsupported tabular export, active-content dependency, external fetch, malformed structure, ambiguous variant, or changed layout, when detection runs, then INGEST returns a stable unsupported or unsafe-source result and creates no committable manifest or LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a source exceeding any advertised adapter bound for size, pages, records, nesting, decompression, memory, or processing time, when preview is attempted, then processing stops with a stable resource-limit result before mutation.
  - AC: Given a proposed new or revised adapter, when it lacks representative fixtures that account for every source record and reconcile every required financial control deterministically, then the variant cannot be advertised as supported.

## Depends On
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Extractor failures use the stable IngestError contract.
- bd-3k6 (TASK-INGEST-GATE-EVIDENCE-PRIVATE-FIXTURES): Fixture-dependent extractor tests consume the validated private expectation manifest.

## In Scope
- Pin the spike-proven upstream package, convert one immutable byte buffer to ordered page/glyph evidence, and define the one real format-adapter seam.
- Produce: Pinned PdfPig dependency.
- Produce: PdfStatementTextExtractor.
- Produce: PdfDocumentEvidence and IStatementAdapter seam.
- Produce: Private fixture injection helper.
- Produce: Native-AOT extraction tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- src/Tally/Tally.csproj pins package PdfPig at exactly 0.1.14 and introduces no legacy UglyToad.PdfPig package, Poppler executable, custom PDF parser, OCR library, or dynamic-code dependency.
- PdfStatementTextExtractor accepts a bounded immutable byte buffer, CancellationToken, and extraction limits and returns ordered PdfDocumentEvidence page/glyph/text/coordinate data with no PdfPig type escaping Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf.
- The extractor never follows links, renders, executes scripts or embedded objects, performs remote fetch, writes extracted text to disk, or modifies the caller-owned source.
- Unsupported opening-password encryption, scan-only content, malformed structure, ambiguous content order, and every configured byte/page/glyph/time bound return a stable pre-mutation IngestError.
- PrivateStatementFixtureSet reads only the path named by TALLY_INGEST_PRIVATE_FIXTURE_MANIFEST; the ignored manifest supplies three source paths, variant IDs, and expected private facts, and neither paths nor facts enter source control or test output.
- All three private fixtures, including the permission-encrypted fixture, extract non-empty evidence twice with identical digests; Release linux-x64 Native-AOT publish succeeds with zero trim/dynamic-code warnings.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use legacy UglyToad.PdfPig 1.7.0-custom-5, Poppler, a child process, or a custom parser — rejected per DD-INGEST-DOCUMENT-EXTRACTION.
- ❌ Do NOT introduce a generic PDF parser interface; PdfStatementTextExtractor remains concrete until a second real production implementation exists.
- ❌ Do NOT place source paths in process arguments, logs, test names, snapshots, or failure messages — per DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY.
- ❌ Do NOT commit fixture bytes, fixture manifests, filenames, expected private facts, or extracted text.

## Approach
Pin the spike-proven upstream package, convert one immutable byte buffer to ordered page/glyph evidence, and define the one real format-adapter seam.
- Constraint: Hard limits are checked before allocation or continued parsing where the source makes that possible; cancellation is propagated at every async boundary.
- Note: The ignored private fixture manifest is external test evidence, not a tracked deliverable.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: IngestError (DM-INGEST-ERROR-STATUS-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: PrivateFixtureExpectationManifest (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT) — Validated by TASK-INGEST-GATE-EVIDENCE-PRIVATE-FIXTURES and supplied via TALLY_INGEST_PRIVATE_FIXTURE_MANIFEST.
- Produces: PdfStatementTextExtractor.ExtractAsync (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Produces: PdfDocumentEvidence (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Produces: IStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Produces: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT)

- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf/PdfStatementTextExtractor.cs` (create) — concrete bounded extractor — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf/IStatementAdapter.cs` (create) — real two-implementation seam — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Fixtures/PrivateStatementFixtureSet.cs` (test) — external ignored fixture injection — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/PdfExtraction/PdfStatementTextExtractorTests.cs` (test) — determinism, bounds, and AOT evidence — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~PdfStatementTextExtractorTests` -> exit 0; at least 12 extraction, malformed-input, encrypted, bound, cancellation, and determinism cases are discovered and 0 tests fail without printing source paths or content

title: Prove the qualified adapter set
description:
## Objective
Prove exclusive detection, deterministic extraction, exact private fixture expectations, and advertised resource bounds for exactly two supported variants.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION: Qualify and detect supported statement variants
  - AC: Given any of the three qualified private fixtures, when variant detection runs, then exactly one of the two versioned text-native PDF variants is selected from structural evidence rather than filename or extension.
  - AC: Given the qualified permission-encrypted fixture that is text-extractable without an opening password, when it is previewed twice, then each run selects exactly the fixture manifest variantId, reproduces every expected ordered record and reconciliation control, requires no opening password, OCR, external process, or external fetch, completes within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory, and creates no LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a scan-only PDF, opening-password requirement, unsupported tabular export, active-content dependency, external fetch, malformed structure, ambiguous variant, or changed layout, when detection runs, then INGEST returns a stable unsupported or unsafe-source result and creates no committable manifest or LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a source exceeding any advertised adapter bound for size, pages, records, nesting, decompression, memory, or processing time, when preview is attempted, then processing stops with a stable resource-limit result before mutation.
  - AC: Given a proposed new or revised adapter, when it lacks representative fixtures that account for every source record and reconcile every required financial control deterministically, then the variant cannot be advertised as supported.

## Depends On
- bd-1as (TASK-INGEST-ADAPTER-LAYOUT-A): The gate consumes PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter.
- bd-3c4 (TASK-INGEST-ADAPTER-LAYOUT-B): The gate consumes PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter.
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): Consumes PdfStatementTextExtractor.ExtractAsync.

## In Scope
- Integration gate converges the extractor and both adapters into the exact explicit v1 registry before preview can consume any format behavior.
- Produce: StatementAdapterRegistry.
- Produce: QualifiedStatementAdapterSet.
- Produce: AdapterQualificationTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- StatementAdapterRegistry explicitly contains exactly one PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter and one PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter in deterministic order; no reflection scan, plugin, fallback, third variant, or user configuration exists.
- Every one of the three private fixtures produces exactly one exact_match and the variant ID declared by the ignored private fixture manifest; zero or multiple matches return a stable unsupported result.
- Each fixture is extracted twice through the real PdfPig extractor and selected adapter with identical ordered source records, dispositions, controls, and safe digests.
- Every transaction-bearing source record is accepted, proven Exact Duplicate by fixture evidence, or blocked; non-transaction exclusions carry stable reasons; all available opening, closing, running-balance, total, and record-count controls match exactly.
- One-over-limit and malformed sources fail before state or LEDGER mutation; measured direct extraction plus adapter work stays below 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak RSS per fixture on the supported release host.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT advertise a format from filename/extension or partial fixture success.
- ❌ Do NOT add OCR, generic tables, regex templates, dynamic plugins, or best-effort fallback — rejected per both governing decisions.
- ❌ Do NOT print or persist private fixture paths, descriptions, amounts, balances, records, or expected facts.

## Approach
Integration gate converges the extractor and both adapters into the exact explicit v1 registry before preview can consume any format behavior.
- Constraint: A new or revised adapter cannot enter the registry without representative private golden evidence and exact reconciliation.

title: Implement passive preview and immutable manifest persistence
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.preview as a deterministic no-LEDGER-mutation workflow that persists only a complete immutable manifest or a stable failure state.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
- FR-INGEST-STATEMENT-PREVIEW: Preview a statement without financial mutation
  - AC: Given a readable supported source and one explicitly selected active canonical account, when preview runs, then INGEST fingerprints the source content, validates statement metadata against the selected account, records adapter and LEDGER contract versions, and returns one stable batch and manifest revision.
  - AC: Given the same Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version, when preview is repeated under any filename, then INGEST identifies Exact Replay and returns the same ordered source-record outcomes, candidate facts, reconciliation evidence, and manifest identity.
  - AC: Given a missing, archived, conflicting, multi-account, or ambiguous selected account, when preview runs, then INGEST blocks the batch rather than inferring an account from the filename or untrusted statement text.
  - AC: Given a source that changes while being read or no longer matches its recorded fingerprint, when preview completes, then INGEST returns a stable source-changed failure and persists no committable manifest.
  - AC: Given any preview success or failure, when the operation ends, then the caller-owned source remains byte-for-byte unchanged and no LEDGER transaction has been created.

## Depends On
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): Preview persists one atomic manifest transaction through IngestDatabase.
- bd-1m0 (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-FORMAT-ADAPTERS): Preview consumes only the qualified StatementAdapterRegistry.

- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Consumes IngestOperationContracts.

## In Scope
- Compose public account lookup, bounded source snapshotting, qualified extraction, domain policies, Exact Replay/overlap checks, and one atomic preview-state transaction.
- Produce: CallerOwnedSourceReader.
- Produce: PreviewStateStore.
- Produce: PreviewHandler.
- Produce: PreviewOperationModule.
- Produce: PreviewWorkflowTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Preview validates contract versions, selected account identifier, path schema, and advertised limits before opening the source; LedgerContractClient confirms exactly one active ZAR account and statement metadata must agree without inference.
- CallerOwnedSourceReader opens read-only, captures safe before/after metadata, reads no more than the byte limit into one immutable buffer, computes SHA-256 over those exact bytes, and returns source_changed if metadata or bytes change.
- PreviewStateStore checks the complete Exact Replay key first: Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version. An active or completed match returns the prior manifest, status, or receipt without reparsing or mutation; any changed key component requires a new preview before overlap evaluation.
- A new source runs the real extractor and exactly one registered adapter, normalizes/accounts/reconciles every record, blocks any ambiguity or overlap, canonicalizes the complete ordered manifest, and persists batch, revision, records, candidates, controls, and safe status in one INGEST transaction.
- Success returns stable batchId and manifestRevisionId; any failure creates no committable manifest; every path leaves the source byte-for-byte unchanged and invokes no ledger.transaction.record.
- PreviewOperationModule exposes a typed handler binding for ingest.preview but is not added to the global registry until GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer the account from filename or masked statement text, reread after manifest freeze, or mutate LEDGER — per C8/C12 and DD-INGEST-MANIFEST-IDENTITY-OVERLAP.
- ❌ Do NOT persist partial candidate/manifests outside one SQLite transaction or retain a source copy/extracted temporary.
- ❌ Do NOT call adapters through reflection/plugins or bypass StatementAdapterRegistry.
- ❌ Do NOT place parsing, reconciliation, identity, or finance policy inside PreviewHandler.

## Approach
Compose public account lookup, bounded source snapshotting, qualified extraction, domain policies, Exact Replay/overlap checks, and one atomic preview-state transaction.
- Constraint: All private payload output is confined to the explicit owner-invoked result envelope; diagnostics stay metadata-only.

## Interfaces

title: Implement safe batch status and failure reporting
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.status with deterministic paging, mutation certainty, unresolved frontier, and safe retry/resume actions without source access.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-FAILURE-STATUS: Report blocked and failed ingestion safely
  - AC: Given any preview, review, commit, resume, or cleanup failure, when the result is returned, then it includes a stable INGEST error code and category, safe batch or candidate identifiers if available, whether any LEDGER mutation may have occurred, the last durable receipt state, and whether retry, resume, or correction is allowed.
  - AC: Given an unsupported, malformed, permission, source-change, resource-limit, compatibility, or reconciliation failure before commit, when status is inspected, then it proves no LEDGER mutation occurred and no committable manifest exists.
  - AC: Given a blocking overlap or ambiguous financial fact, when status is inspected, then it identifies the blocked source-context record safely and requires a new reviewed resolution rather than automatic continuation.
  - AC: Given a commit-stage failure after some Terminal Candidate Outcomes, when status is inspected, then it identifies the same approved manifest, completed outcomes, unresolved frontier, and safe resume operation.
  - AC: Given any expected domain failure, when diagnostics are emitted, then stdout contains one structured result, stderr contains metadata only, and no stack trace or parser exception substitutes for the stable contract.

## Depends On
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Status consumes IngestError, BatchStatus, and ImportReceipt contracts.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): StatusStateStore reads the V001 INGEST schema.

## In Scope
- Read one detailed batch or a stable cursor page of retained summaries and map every expected failure to metadata-only durable-state guidance.
- Produce: StatusQuery/Handler.
- Produce: StatusStateStore.
- Produce: StatusOperationModule.
- Produce: StatusWorkflowTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- With batchId, status returns BatchStatusDetail for the exact batch: safe summary, manifest revision, approval, receipt state, terminal counts, unresolved frontier, last stable error, retained artifact kinds, and next allowed operations without rereading the source.
- Without batchId, limit defaults to 50, accepts only 1 through 100, cursor is opaque and tamper-rejected, results order by updatedAt descending then batchId, and nextCursor neither skips nor duplicates rows under a stable snapshot.
- Pre-commit source/permission/resource/compatibility/reconciliation failures report mutationPossibility=none and no committable manifest; overlap identifies only safe source-context IDs and requires repreview.
- Commit-stage failures report mutationPossibility=possible or confirmed from durable evidence, the exact approved manifest, completed outcomes, unresolved frontier, and resume or abandon guidance.
- Every expected failure uses one stable IngestError code/category/retryAction; stdout has one structured result and diagnostics never contain stack trace, parser exception, path, row, description, amount, balance, full identifier, manifest, or request.

title: Implement deterministic identity and reconciliation policies
description:
## Objective
Turn qualified source evidence into deterministic immutable candidate meaning without filesystem, SQLite, PdfPig, or LEDGER side effects.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-FINANCIAL-NORMALIZATION: Normalize exact source financial facts
  - AC: Given a transaction-bearing source record with complete evidence, when it is normalized, then the candidate preserves the selected account, original description, exact base-10 amount, ZAR, transaction date, optional distinct posting date, adapter version, source fingerprint, source-context record identity, and source reference without precision loss.
  - AC: Given an asset or liability statement movement, when its signed amount is normalized, then positive means the owner economic position improved and negative means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
  - AC: Given a source transaction date that includes its year, when it is normalized, then that exact calendar date is the Effective Date.
  - AC: Given a source row with zero monetary movement, when it is accounted for, then it is retained as an explicitly excluded non-transaction record with a stable reason and no zero-amount LEDGER candidate is created.
  - AC: Given a missing or ambiguous amount, sign, currency, date, description, record boundary, account meaning, or provenance field, when normalization is attempted, then the source record is blocked without inventing or editing a value.
  - AC: Given a row date without a year in the qualified yearless layout, when exactly one matching calendar date falls inside the explicit statement period, then that date is the Effective Date; otherwise the batch is blocked.
- FR-INGEST-REPLAY-OVERLAP-SAFETY: Handle exact replay and uncertain overlap safely
  - AC: Given the same Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version, when a source is previewed again under any filename, then INGEST identifies Exact Replay and returns the prior batch or receipt without new LEDGER mutation.
  - AC: Given two identical date-description-amount records at different source positions in one statement, when they are normalized, then both remain distinct source-context candidates and tuple equality alone never collapses either record.
  - AC: Given a prior candidate with the same variant-defined identity and the same immutable financial facts, when a new supported source proves that identity, then the candidate is reported as an Exact Duplicate and is not written again.
  - AC: Given an overlapping window or similar amount, date, or description without identity-grade evidence, when preview runs, then INGEST emits a blocking Overlap Conflict and does not claim a duplicate.
  - AC: Given reuse of an exact identity with different immutable facts, when preview or commit checks it, then INGEST returns a stable conflict, preserves the original effect, and makes no new mutation for that candidate.
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.

## Depends On
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Pure policies construct the canonical manifest and error contract records.

## In Scope
- Create pure finance/domain policies for exact normalization, stable identity, source-record accounting, reconciliation, canonical manifests, Exact Replay, and fail-closed overlap.
- Produce: IngestIdentity.
- Produce: FinancialNormalizer.
- Produce: StatementReconciler.
- Produce: ManifestCanonicalizer.
- Produce: OverlapPolicy.
- Produce: Pure domain tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- FinancialNormalizer converts exact base-10 evidence to integer minor units and owner-economic sign: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative; currency is exactly ZAR.
- TransactionDate is the source date with its year; a yearless date resolves only when exactly one date lies inside the explicit statement period; PostingDate remains distinct and optional; missing or ambiguous facts block without invention.
- Zero monetary movement becomes excluded_non_transaction with a stable reason and never an ImportCandidate.
- IngestIdentity derives sourceRecordId from Source Fingerprint, stable structural position, raw evidence fingerprint, and immutable-facts schema while excluding adapter/manifest/compatible-version metadata; candidateId derives from accountId, sourceRecordId, and immutable normalized facts and drives sourceReference/idempotencyKey.
- Identical tuples at different positions remain distinct; filename and fuzzy similarity never affect identity; exact identity with changed immutable facts is conflict.
- StatementReconciler accounts for every record exactly once and verifies opening plus movements equals closing plus every available running/source control in integer minor units; missing controls are unavailable, not fabricated.
- ManifestCanonicalizer emits source-generated canonical JSON and manifestRevisionId deterministically; OverlapPolicy uses the complete Exact Replay key—Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version—to return prior state before parsing; any changed key component creates a new preview, and different-source overlapping windows remain blocked because v1 has no cross-file identity.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use random IDs, filename identity, date-description-amount tuple collapse, fuzzy similarity, or nearest-date matching — rejected per DD-INGEST-MANIFEST-IDENTITY-OVERLAP.
- ❌ Do NOT include manifest revision, adapter version, executable version, or compatible Ledger version in sourceRecordId or candidateId.
- ❌ Do NOT use floating point, edit source facts, infer a missing account/date/sign/currency, or fabricate unavailable controls.
- ❌ Do NOT add filesystem, SQLite, PdfPig, OperationRegistry, or Ledger calls to Domain/Ingest.

## Approach
Create pure finance/domain policies for exact normalization, stable identity, source-record accounting, reconciliation, canonical manifests, Exact Replay, and fail-closed overlap.
- Constraint: All hashes use an explicit versioned canonical byte representation; all state and disposition enums are closed system-owned types.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: ImportManifestContract (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Produces: IngestIdentity (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Produces: FinancialNormalizer (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Produces: StatementReconciler (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Produces: ManifestCanonicalizer (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Produces: OverlapPolicy (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ingest/Identity/IngestIdentity.cs` (create) — stable batch/record/candidate identities — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ingest/Normalization/FinancialNormalizer.cs` (create) — money, sign, date, provenance normalization — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ingest/Reconciliation/StatementReconciler.cs` (create) — record accounting and controls — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ingest/Manifests/ManifestCanonicalizer.cs` (create) — canonical manifest digest — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ingest/Overlap/OverlapPolicy.cs` (create) — exact replay and blocked overlap policy — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Identity/IngestIdentityTests.cs` (test) — identity collision and stability tests — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Preview/FinancialNormalizationTests.cs` (test) — money/sign/date tests — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Preview/ReconciliationAndManifestTests.cs` (test) — accounting, controls, canonicalization, overlap tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~IngestIdentityTests|FullyQualifiedName~FinancialNormalizationTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReconciliationAndManifestTests'` -> exit 0; at least 35 identity, tuple-collision, sign, date, zero-movement, accounting, control, canonicalization, exact-replay, and overlap cases are discovered and 0 tests fail

title: Implement the idempotent candidate commit saga
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.commit so every candidate is accepted once, returned as an exact prior result, or durably stopped without unsafe continuation.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-APPROVED-BATCH-COMMIT: Commit approved candidates through public LEDGER operations
  - AC: Given an approved immutable manifest revision whose adapter and discovered LEDGER versions remain compatible, when commit starts, then INGEST validates its fingerprint, approval, versions, account state, and candidate identities before the first mutation.
  - AC: Given an accepted candidate, when it is committed, then INGEST invokes only the public LEDGER transaction-recording operation with the frozen request, statement-import provenance, and a stable candidate idempotency identity.
  - AC: Given an Exact Duplicate outcome, when commit traverses the manifest, then INGEST records the prior canonical reference and invokes no new LEDGER write for that candidate.
  - AC: Given a successful LEDGER result, when INGEST records the outcome, then the returned stable transaction reference round-trips through the public LEDGER read contract with the approved immutable facts.
  - AC: Given a LEDGER validation, compatibility, or idempotency conflict for a candidate, when commit receives it, then INGEST durably records the stable outcome and stops before unsafe continuation.
- FR-INGEST-DURABLE-RECEIPT-RESUME: Record durable outcomes and resume interrupted commit
  - AC: Given an approved batch commit, when any candidate receives a structured LEDGER result, then INGEST durably records its accepted, Exact Duplicate, conflicted, rejected, or unresolved receipt outcome before attempting the next candidate.
  - AC: Given interruption after a durable receipt outcome, when resume runs, then it skips terminal candidates and reuses the exact frozen request and original idempotency identity only for unresolved candidates.
  - AC: Given interruption after LEDGER committed but before INGEST recorded the outcome, when resume replays the original request and identity, then LEDGER returns the original result and INGEST records it without a second transaction.
  - AC: Given any resume or status request, when it runs, then it uses the frozen manifest and durable receipt rather than rereading or reparsing the caller-owned source.
  - AC: Given every candidate has a Terminal Candidate Outcome, when commit or resume completes, then INGEST returns one complete receipt with exact outcome counts and canonical references and marks no candidate merely processed.

## Depends On
- bd-37p (TASK-INGEST-REVIEW-WORKFLOW): Commit consumes exact immutable approvals and manifests from ReviewStateStore.
- bd-2sg (TASK-INGEST-LEDGER-PUBLIC-CLIENT): The saga invokes only LedgerContractClient record/get methods.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): CommitStateStore and BatchCommitLock require protected ingest.db and artifact primitives.
- bd-2gk (TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-DOMAIN): Commit revalidates stable candidate identity and canonical manifest digest.
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Consumes ImportReceipt and FrozenLedgerRecordRequest.

## In Scope
- Commit one approved frozen candidate at a time under a per-batch OS lock, with durable attempting/terminal states bracketing only public LEDGER calls.
- Produce: CandidateCommitState.
- Produce: BatchCommitLock.
- Produce: CommitStateStore.
- Produce: CandidateCommitSaga.
- Produce: CommitOperationModule.
- Produce: Commit saga tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Commit validates exact batch, manifest revision/digest, active approval, compatible adapter/manifest/Ledger versions, active selected account, candidate identity, and canonical manifest integrity before acquiring the lock or causing the first mutation.
- BatchCommitLock is owner-only, non-reentrant across processes, rejects concurrent commit/resume for one batch, and is released by process loss; different batches may proceed independently.
- For each frozen accepted candidate in deterministic manifest order, CommitStateStore durably records attempting plus the exact request and idempotency key before LedgerContractClient.RecordTransactionAsync runs outside any INGEST transaction.
- A successful record result becomes terminal only after GetTransactionAsync round-trips account, signed amount, currency, dates, description, source reference, provenance, and transaction ID exactly and CommitStateStore durably records accepted before the next candidate.
- An exact_duplicate candidate records its prior canonical reference and invokes no write; a stable Ledger validation, compatibility, idempotency, round-trip, or receipt-write conflict records the safe frontier and stops continuation.
- When every candidate is terminal, the complete ImportReceipt reports exact counts/canonical references and verified completion; no candidate is labeled merely processed.
- CommitOperationModule binds ingest.commit but is not globally registered until the public-contract gate.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use a whole-batch/distributed transaction, hold SQLite across a Ledger call, or keep progress only in memory — rejected per DD-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY.
- ❌ Do NOT regenerate a request/key, reread the source, use a new manifest revision, or skip public transaction.get verification.
- ❌ Do NOT call private Ledger storage/handlers or continue after an unsafe/conflicting result.
- ❌ Do NOT mark attempting as terminal or advance before durable receipt state.

## Approach
Commit one approved frozen candidate at a time under a per-batch OS lock, with durable attempting/terminal states bracketing only public LEDGER calls.
- Constraint: All candidate ordering, request bytes, keys, contract versions, and manifest meaning are frozen before the first Ledger call.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: ImportReceipt (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-RECEIPT)
- Consumes: FrozenLedgerRecordRequest (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT)
- Consumes: IngestDatabase (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Consumes: ReviewStateStore (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Consumes: LedgerContractClient.RecordTransactionAsync (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT)
- Consumes: LedgerContractClient.GetTransactionAsync (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT)
- Produces: BatchCommitLock (DD-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY)
- Produces: CommitStateStore (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Produces: CandidateCommitSaga.ExecuteAsync (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-RECEIPT)
- Produces: CommitOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)

title: Implement immutable inspect and approval
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.inspect and ingest.approve with immutable revision, compatibility, actor, and approval invalidation guarantees.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-MANIFEST-REVIEW: Review and approve an immutable manifest revision
  - AC: Given a completed preview, when the manifest is inspected, then it exposes safe batch identity, source fingerprint, selected account, adapter and LEDGER contract versions, ordered source-record outcomes, candidate facts, exclusions, exact duplicates, overlap conflicts, validation results, and reconciliation evidence.
  - AC: Given a manifest whose transaction-bearing records are all accepted or proven exact duplicates and whose required controls reconcile, when the owner explicitly approves that exact revision, then its approval state and actor are recorded without LEDGER mutation.
  - AC: Given any blocked record, unresolved overlap, failed control, incompatible version, or missing required fact, when approval is attempted, then INGEST rejects approval and preserves the uncommittable manifest.
  - AC: Given an attempted change to amount, currency, date, original description, sign, or provenance, when review is performed, then INGEST rejects inline editing and requires a corrected source or adapter plus a new preview.
  - AC: Given a changed account decision, duplicate disposition, adapter version, or LEDGER contract version, when the batch is reconsidered, then INGEST creates a new manifest revision and invalidates approval of prior revisions for commit.

## Depends On
- bd-1of (TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-WORKFLOW): Inspect and approve consume persisted immutable manifests created by preview.
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Consumes ImportManifestContract.
- bd-2gk (TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-DOMAIN): Consumes ManifestCanonicalizer.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): Consumes IngestDatabase.

## In Scope
- Expose the exact sensitive manifest to its owner and record approval only for one committable digest without editing source-derived facts or mutating LEDGER.
- Produce: InspectHandler.
- Produce: ApproveHandler.
- Produce: ReviewStateStore.
- Produce: ReviewOperationModule.
- Produce: ReviewWorkflowTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- ingest.inspect requires exact batchId plus manifestRevisionId and returns the complete owner-only manifest view: identities, versions, selected account, ordered record outcomes, immutable candidates, exclusions, duplicates, conflicts, controls, validations, and approval state.
- Inspect performs no mutation and deterministic repeated reads return semantically identical ordered content.
- Approve rehashes the stored canonical manifest, checks exact digest/revision, committable state, every record/control, active selected account, compatible adapter/manifest/Ledger versions, and trusted actor attribution before one atomic approval insert.
- Any blocked record, overlap, failed control, incompatible version, missing fact, digest mismatch, stale account, or absent actor returns a stable failure and leaves the revision unapproved.
- Requests cannot update amount, currency, date, originalDescription, sign, provenance, account, adapter, duplicate disposition, or version; changed meaning requires a new preview/revision and prior approval is inactive for commit.
- ReviewOperationModule produces inspect/approve descriptor bindings but global registration waits for GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT implement a generic save/update or inline manifest editor — rejected by DD-INGEST-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT and DD-INGEST-MANIFEST-IDENTITY-OVERLAP.
- ❌ Do NOT approve by batchId alone, mutable latest revision, or unchecked digest.
- ❌ Do NOT mutate Ledger or reread/reparse the source during inspect or approve.
- ❌ Do NOT log or send the sensitive manifest to stderr/telemetry; it is allowed only in explicit stdout business output.

## Approach
Expose the exact sensitive manifest to its owner and record approval only for one committable digest without editing source-derived facts or mutating LEDGER.
- Constraint: Inspect is a query; approve is a narrow CommandResult transition with expected domain failures, not an EF/HTTP CRUD save.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: ImportManifestContract (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Consumes: IngestDatabase (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Consumes: ManifestCanonicalizer (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)

- Produces: ReviewStateStore (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Produces: ReviewOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)

- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/InspectHandler.cs` (create) — sensitive owner result mapping — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/ApproveCommand.cs` (create) — exact revision transition — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/ApproveHandler.cs` (create) — approval orchestration — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/ReviewOperationModule.cs` (create) — inspect/approve descriptor bindings — required

- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Review/ReviewWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — inspect/approval state tests — required

## Verification

title: Implement the qualified layout B adapter
description:
## Objective
Produce deterministic layout B statement evidence and normalized source records without a generic template or fallback parser.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-FINANCIAL-NORMALIZATION: Normalize exact source financial facts
  - AC: Given a transaction-bearing source record with complete evidence, when it is normalized, then the candidate preserves the selected account, original description, exact base-10 amount, ZAR, transaction date, optional distinct posting date, adapter version, source fingerprint, source-context record identity, and source reference without precision loss.
  - AC: Given an asset or liability statement movement, when its signed amount is normalized, then positive means the owner economic position improved and negative means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
  - AC: Given a source transaction date that includes its year, when it is normalized, then that exact calendar date is the Effective Date.
  - AC: Given a source row with zero monetary movement, when it is accounted for, then it is retained as an explicitly excluded non-transaction record with a stable reason and no zero-amount LEDGER candidate is created.
  - AC: Given a missing or ambiguous amount, sign, currency, date, description, record boundary, account meaning, or provenance field, when normalization is attempted, then the source record is blocked without inventing or editing a value.
  - AC: Given a row date without a year in the qualified yearless layout, when exactly one matching calendar date falls inside the explicit statement period, then that date is the Effective Date; otherwise the batch is blocked.
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION: Qualify and detect supported statement variants
  - AC: Given any of the three qualified private fixtures, when variant detection runs, then exactly one of the two versioned text-native PDF variants is selected from structural evidence rather than filename or extension.
  - AC: Given the qualified permission-encrypted fixture that is text-extractable without an opening password, when it is previewed twice, then each run selects exactly the fixture manifest variantId, reproduces every expected ordered record and reconciliation control, requires no opening password, OCR, external process, or external fetch, completes within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory, and creates no LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a scan-only PDF, opening-password requirement, unsupported tabular export, active-content dependency, external fetch, malformed structure, ambiguous variant, or changed layout, when detection runs, then INGEST returns a stable unsupported or unsafe-source result and creates no committable manifest or LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a source exceeding any advertised adapter bound for size, pages, records, nesting, decompression, memory, or processing time, when preview is attempted, then processing stops with a stable resource-limit result before mutation.
  - AC: Given a proposed new or revised adapter, when it lacks representative fixtures that account for every source record and reconcile every required financial control deterministically, then the variant cannot be advertised as supported.

## Depends On
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): The adapter implements IStatementAdapter over PdfDocumentEvidence and uses the private fixture helper.

## In Scope
- Encode only the structural detection, row boundaries, financial semantics, exclusions, and source controls proven by the private layout B fixture, including permission encryption and yearless dates.
- Produce: PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter.
- Produce: LayoutBStatementAdapterTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Descriptor advertises exactly pdf-text-layout-b-v1, its adapter/extraction/manifest versions, media type, and hard limits.
- Probe selects exact_match only from layout B structural evidence, including the permission-encrypted but opening-password-free fixture, and returns no_match for layout A and unsupported fixtures.
- Extract accounts for every layout B source record in order, preserves raw evidence privately, distinguishes transaction and non-transaction records, and exposes all variant controls.
- A yearless source date resolves only when exactly one calendar date lies inside the explicit statement period; zero or multiple matches block the record and batch.
- Layout B account-class, owner-economic sign, zero-movement exclusion, amount, description, source-reference, and control rules reproduce every private expected fact in integer minor units.
- Two runs produce identical ordered evidence and structural positions; password-required, scan-only, malformed, or drifted structures fail closed.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add conditional branches for layout A or future banks — rejected single-parser alternative per DD-INGEST-FORMAT-ADAPTERS.
- ❌ Do NOT guess a year, use filename/extension, fuzzy-match rows, or fall back to OCR/template configuration.
- ❌ Do NOT commit private fixtures, expected facts, filenames, extracted text, or payload snapshots.

## Approach
Encode only the structural detection, row boundaries, financial semantics, exclusions, and source controls proven by the private layout B fixture, including permission encryption and yearless dates.
- Constraint: All private expected facts come through PrivateStatementFixtureSet and remain outside source control.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: IStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PdfDocumentEvidence (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT)
- Produces: PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)

## Verification

title: Implement deterministic interrupted-commit resume
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.resume so repeated recovery converges without a second canonical transaction or a changed request.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-DURABLE-RECEIPT-RESUME: Record durable outcomes and resume interrupted commit
  - AC: Given an approved batch commit, when any candidate receives a structured LEDGER result, then INGEST durably records its accepted, Exact Duplicate, conflicted, rejected, or unresolved receipt outcome before attempting the next candidate.
  - AC: Given interruption after a durable receipt outcome, when resume runs, then it skips terminal candidates and reuses the exact frozen request and original idempotency identity only for unresolved candidates.
  - AC: Given interruption after LEDGER committed but before INGEST recorded the outcome, when resume replays the original request and identity, then LEDGER returns the original result and INGEST records it without a second transaction.
  - AC: Given any resume or status request, when it runs, then it uses the frozen manifest and durable receipt rather than rereading or reparsing the caller-owned source.
  - AC: Given every candidate has a Terminal Candidate Outcome, when commit or resume completes, then INGEST returns one complete receipt with exact outcome counts and canonical references and marks no candidate merely processed.

## Depends On
- bd-2i6 (TASK-INGEST-COMMIT-SAGA): Resume reuses CandidateCommitSaga, BatchCommitLock, and CommitStateStore.

## In Scope
- Reuse CandidateCommitSaga for only unresolved frozen requests and prove every before/after Ledger and receipt-durability crash window.
- Produce: ResumeHandler.
- Produce: ResumeOperationModule.
- Produce: Fault-injection crash matrix tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Resume requires one batchId, acquires the same BatchCommitLock, loads only the frozen approved manifest/requests/keys and durable receipt, and never rereads or reparses the source.
- Terminal accepted, exact_duplicate, conflicted, and rejected candidates are skipped only after any stored canonical reference still validates; pending, attempting, or unresolved candidates replay the exact stored request/key in original order.
- Fault injection before Ledger call, after Ledger commit, before INGEST receipt durability, after receipt durability, and between candidates produces a recoverable durable frontier and stops further candidates.
- A crash after Ledger commit but before receipt durability replays the original key and records the original public result with no second transaction.
- Changed request bytes, manifest digest, candidate identity, adapter/manifest/Ledger compatibility, canonical detail, or reused key with changed facts returns stable conflict and does not continue.
- Repeated resume converges to one complete receipt with exact terminal counts; concurrent resume/commit is rejected by the per-batch lock.
- ResumeOperationModule binds ingest.resume but global registration waits for the public-contract gate.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT generate a new idempotency key/request, choose a latest manifest, reread the source, retry terminal candidates blindly, or proceed after mismatch.
- ❌ Do NOT add a second recovery engine; call CandidateCommitSaga.ExecuteAsync with resume mode — per DD-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY.
- ❌ Do NOT use an in-memory cursor or hold a database transaction across public Ledger execution.

## Approach
Reuse CandidateCommitSaga for only unresolved frozen requests and prove every before/after Ledger and receipt-durability crash window.
- Constraint: Fault hooks are internal test seams only and must not become public configuration or production plugin ports.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CandidateCommitSaga.ExecuteAsync (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-RECEIPT)
- Consumes: BatchCommitLock (DD-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY)
- Consumes: CommitStateStore (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)

description:

title: Create the protected INGEST SQLite foundation
description:
## Objective
Provide an owner-only ingest.db with deterministic schema evolution and no access path to LedgerDb.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP: Abandon and clean up ingestion artifacts safely
  - AC: Given preview, commit, failure, or abandonment reaches a safe boundary, when temporary extraction data is no longer required, then INGEST removes it deterministically and retains no caller-owned source copy.
  - AC: Given a crash left known temporary artifacts, when the next safe INGEST invocation starts, then cleanup identifies and removes only orphaned INGEST temporaries without touching sources, manifests required for resume, receipts, or LEDGER data.
  - AC: Given an incomplete batch, when the owner explicitly abandons it, then INGEST records abandonment, prevents future commit or resume for that manifest revision, removes disposable temporaries, leaves prior canonical transactions unchanged, and leaves the source untouched.
  - AC: Given an incomplete manifest or receipt still required for review or resume, when cleanup is requested, then INGEST rejects destructive cleanup with a stable retained-for-recovery result.
  - AC: Given a completed metadata-only receipt or safely abandoned batch, when the owner explicitly requests cleanup, then INGEST removes only the selected retained INGEST artifacts and reports the result without deleting any canonical transaction or caller-owned source.

## Depends On
- bd-2dg (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT): The shared Tally solution and data-root conventions must exist before the separate INGEST store is added.

## In Scope
- Create only the separate raw-SQLite connection, immutable V001 schema, migration runner, and owner-only artifact enforcement used by feature-owned SQL stores.
- Produce: IngestDatabase.
- Produce: IngestMigrationV001 and migrator.
- Produce: IngestArtifactProtection.
- Produce: Real-SQLite foundation tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- IngestDatabase opens only <TallyDataRoot>/ingest/ingest.db, enables foreign_keys, WAL, synchronous=FULL, and bounded busy handling, and never opens or attaches LedgerDb.
- IngestMigrationV001 creates every table, key, uniqueness rule, and relationship in DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE with snake_case identifiers; PRAGMA user_version advances atomically from 0 to 1.
- Repeated migration is idempotent, unknown newer user_version fails with a stable compatibility result, and interruption leaves either the complete prior or complete V001 schema.
- IngestArtifactProtection creates directories as 0700 and state, WAL, SHM, lock, and atomic artifacts as 0600 where supported; inability to establish protection blocks persistence.
- Real temporary SQLite tests enforce immutable revision numbering, unique idempotency keys, foreign keys, and concurrent writer bounds; architecture guards find no LedgerDb or private LEDGER storage reference.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT share LedgerDb or LEDGER tables, attach databases, or use private LEDGER storage — rejected per DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE.
- ❌ Do NOT replace raw Microsoft.Data.Sqlite with EF Core, PostgreSQL, JSON batch files, or a generic repository — rejected per DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE and DD-INGEST-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- ❌ Do NOT persist when owner-only permissions cannot be established — per DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY.
- ❌ Do NOT modify V001 after release; add a new immutable migration version.

## Approach
Create only the separate raw-SQLite connection, immutable V001 schema, migration runner, and owner-only artifact enforcement used by feature-owned SQL stores.
- Constraint: Use explicit SQL transactions and propagate CancellationToken through every asynchronous SQLite call.

## Interfaces

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP: Abandon and clean up ingestion artifacts safely
  - AC: Given preview, commit, failure, or abandonment reaches a safe boundary, when temporary extraction data is no longer required, then INGEST removes it deterministically and retains no caller-owned source copy.
  - AC: Given a crash left known temporary artifacts, when the next safe INGEST invocation starts, then cleanup identifies and removes only orphaned INGEST temporaries without touching sources, manifests required for resume, receipts, or LEDGER data.
  - AC: Given an incomplete batch, when the owner explicitly abandons it, then INGEST records abandonment, prevents future commit or resume for that manifest revision, removes disposable temporaries, leaves prior canonical transactions unchanged, and leaves the source untouched.
  - AC: Given an incomplete manifest or receipt still required for review or resume, when cleanup is requested, then INGEST rejects destructive cleanup with a stable retained-for-recovery result.
  - AC: Given a completed metadata-only receipt or safely abandoned batch, when the owner explicitly requests cleanup, then INGEST removes only the selected retained INGEST artifacts and reports the result without deleting any canonical transaction or caller-owned source.

## Depends On
- bd-2i6 (TASK-INGEST-COMMIT-SAGA): Abandon and compaction consume BatchCommitLock and durable candidate outcomes.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): RecoveryStateStore uses protected ingest.db and artifact enforcement.
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Consumes ImportReceipt.

## In Scope
- Make incomplete state terminal only after lock release, preserve prior canonical effects, compact sensitive state, and delete only state-proven disposable INGEST artifacts.
- Produce: Abandon and cleanup handlers.
- Produce: RecoveryStateStore.
- Produce: StartupIngestCleanup.
- Produce: RecoveryCleanupOperationModule.
- Produce: Cleanup tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Abandon requires an incomplete non-terminal batch, exact durable state, explicit reason, and an unheld BatchCommitLock; it atomically marks abandoned, blocks future commit/resume, preserves prior Ledger transaction references/counts, and compacts sensitive state to a metadata tombstone.
- A completed batch compacts manifest rows, descriptions, amounts, balances, controls, evidence, and frozen requests only after every terminal canonical reference verifies; CompletedMetadataReceipt retains stable identities, versions, outcome categories, safe refs, Ledger refs, counts, and completedAt.
- Cleanup rejects active, approved, committing, interrupted-but-recoverable, locked, ambiguous, or mismatched expectedTerminalStatus targets with retained_for_recovery; it removes only the selected completed receipt or abandoned tombstone/artifacts.
- StartupIngestCleanup inspects only known INGEST lock/atomic artifact names, verifies no live lock and durable disposability, and removes stale artifacts; unknown files, sources, manifests required for resume, receipts, and Ledger data are untouched.
- Every success, expected failure, simulated crash, abandon, cleanup, and startup path leaves no extraction temporary and leaves caller-owned source bytes unchanged.
- RecoveryCleanupOperationModule binds ingest.abandon and ingest.cleanup but global registration waits for the public-contract gate.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT cascade-delete canonical Ledger effects, caller-owned sources, active/recoverable state, or unknown files — RESTRICT by default per ADR-CORE-0020 and DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE.
- ❌ Do NOT abandon while the batch lock is held or erase evidence needed to describe prior Ledger effects.
- ❌ Do NOT use broad globs, directory recursion, filename similarity, or age alone to choose cleanup targets.
- ❌ Do NOT create extraction temporary as part of normal PdfPig processing.

## Approach
Make incomplete state terminal only after lock release, preserve prior canonical effects, compact sensitive state, and delete only state-proven disposable INGEST artifacts.
- Constraint: Deletion targets are explicit state-derived paths beneath the validated INGEST data root; sources and Ledger paths are never eligible.
- Note: Removal impact: completed sensitive state is replaced by a metadata-only receipt; abandoned sensitive state is replaced by a terminal tombstone, so replay prevention and prior-effect evidence are retained.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: ImportReceipt (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-RECEIPT)
- Consumes: IngestDatabase (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Consumes: IngestArtifactProtection.EnsureOwnerOnly (DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY)
