title: Verify replay and overlap safety workflow
description:
## Objective
Prove repeated or overlapping statements never create an unjustified duplicate suppression or second canonical transaction.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-REPLAY-OVERLAP-SAFETY-CONTRACT: Verify replay and overlap safety (verifies).
- Proves or constrains UC-INGEST-004: Re-import a repeated or overlapping statement safely (covers).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC verification exercises the complete published preview/commit/status contract.
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): Consumes PrivateStatementFixtureSet.

## In Scope
- Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-004 Exact Replay, distinct tuple, identity conflict, and fail-closed overlap behavior.
- Produce: UCIngest004Evidence.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- The same Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version under any filename returns the prior active or completed batch or receipt before reparsing and creates no new Ledger effect; changing any key component requires a new preview.
- A familiar filename with changed bytes is a new preview, never Exact Replay.
- Identical date-description-amount rows at distinct source positions remain separate candidates; tuple equality never collapses them.
- Only variant-proven exact identity with equal immutable facts may be Exact Duplicate; unavailable/incompatible provenance never claims automatic deduplication.
- An exact identity reused with changed immutable facts returns stable conflict and preserves the original transaction.
- A different source with an overlapping account/date window and no cross-file identity returns blocking Overlap Conflict and remains uncommitted.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT alter private fixtures or tracked expectations to manufacture identity evidence.
- ❌ Do NOT inspect internal hashes/stores; use public preview/status/receipt and Ledger observations.

## Approach
Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-004 Exact Replay, distinct tuple, identity conflict, and fail-closed overlap behavior.
- Constraint: OQ-INGEST-4 remains fail-closed: no v1 test may infer cross-file identity from similarity.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Published INGEST and shared Ledger operations
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT) — External private fixture injection
- Produces: UCIngest004Evidence (UC-INGEST-004) — Replay and overlap workflow proof

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/E2E/ReplayOverlapWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — UC-INGEST-004 black-box gate — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ReplayOverlapWorkflowTests` -> exit 0; at least 10 renamed-replay, changed-content, duplicate-tuple, exact-identity, incompatible-provenance, changed-facts, and overlap cases are discovered and 0 tests fail with unchanged canonical counts on blocked/replay paths

title: Define the INGEST operation and business contracts
description:
## Objective
Produce the typed Native-AOT-safe contract foundation for all eight INGEST operations without registering incomplete handlers.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY: Discover the INGEST command contract
  - AC: Given a compatible Tally executable, when the client requests INGEST help or schema discovery, then it returns the versioned operations preview, inspect, approve, commit, resume, status, abandon, and cleanup with their input/result schemas, supported Format Variants, safe limits, stable errors, and exit categories without reading a source or mutating LEDGER.
  - AC: Given any INGEST operation, when a valid request completes, then exactly one structured business result is written to stdout, metadata-only diagnostics use stderr, and the documented exit category matches the result.
  - AC: Given an unknown operation, malformed input, or forbidden unknown field, when invocation is attempted, then INGEST returns a stable usage or validation error before source processing or LEDGER mutation.
  - AC: Given incompatible INGEST, adapter, or LEDGER contract versions, when compatibility is checked, then INGEST returns a stable compatibility failure before source processing or mutation.

## Depends On
- bd-2dg (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT): The common envelope and public Ledger contract types must exist before INGEST contract records compile.

## In Scope
- Create source-generated public records, stable errors, operation IDs, manifest/receipt views, and compatibility metadata used identically by every later slice.
- Produce: Eight-operation contract set.
- Produce: Stable errors and status records.
- Produce: Manifest and receipt contracts.
- Produce: Source-generated IngestJsonContext.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- IngestOperationIds contains exactly ingest.preview, ingest.inspect, ingest.approve, ingest.commit, ingest.resume, ingest.status, ingest.abandon, and ingest.cleanup with no generic operation.
- Concrete request/result records match DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS, use closed enums for finite states, carry explicit contract versions, and expose sourcePath only inside PreviewRequest.
- IngestError exposes category, mutationPossibility none|possible|confirmed, retryAction, safe identifiers, and durable state but cannot carry source paths, rows, amounts, balances, full bank identifiers, raw requests, manifests, stack traces, or parser exceptions.
- Manifest and receipt records preserve stable identifiers and immutable facts while CompletedMetadataReceipt excludes descriptions, amounts, balances, evidence, controls, and frozen requests.
- IngestJsonContext contains source-generated metadata for every contract type, rejects unknown fields, and produces byte-identical logical JSON across repeated serialization.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT hide transitions behind a generic action discriminator — rejected per DD-INGEST-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT introduce HTTP DTOs, FastEndpoints, controllers, mutable CRUD DTOs, or UI models — rejected per DD-INGEST-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- ❌ Do NOT expose SQLite, PdfPig, private LEDGER, filesystem, exception, or parser types in public contracts.
- ❌ Do NOT use reflection-based JSON metadata or runtime property-order hashing.

## Approach
Create source-generated public records, stable errors, operation IDs, manifest/receipt views, and compatibility metadata used identically by every later slice.
- Constraint: All complex requests use the common structured input envelope; sourcePath is never a named command-line argument.

## Interfaces
- Produces: IngestOperationIds (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: IngestOperationContracts (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: IngestError (DM-INGEST-ERROR-STATUS-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: BatchStatusSummary (DM-INGEST-ERROR-STATUS-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: ImportManifestContract (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Produces: ImportReceipt (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-RECEIPT)
- Produces: FrozenLedgerRecordRequest (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT)
- Produces: IngestJsonContext (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)

## Files (reservation globs)

- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestErrorContracts.cs` (create) — stable errors and status records — required
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestManifestContracts.cs` (create) — manifest and candidate views — required
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestReceiptContracts.cs` (create) — receipt and frozen Ledger records — required
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestJsonContext.cs` (create) — source-generated JSON metadata — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Contract/IngestOperationIds.cs` (create) — stable operation/version constants — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Contract/IngestContractModelTests.cs` (test) — contract determinism tests — required

## Verification
